conversation:
Client: We need a website for our cafe where customers can see the menu and opening hours.
Client: Customers should be able to reserve tables online.
PM: Should staff update menu items themselves?
Client: Yes, an admin page should let staff edit menu items and prices.
Client: The site must load fast on mobile because most customers use phones.
Client: I want a clean and modern design, and maybe online payment later.
PM: Do you already have a preferred payment provider?

gold.project_summary: Cafe needs a customer-facing website with reservations and admin menu management, with mobile performance and unresolved payment details.

functional_requirements.text: Customers can view the cafe menu and opening hours.
Customers can reserve tables online.
Staff can edit menu items and prices through an admin page.

non_functional_requirements.text: Website should load quickly on mobile devices.
Interface should have a clean and modern design.

constraints.text: Initial release should prioritize mobile usage patterns.

assumptions.text: Online payment is out of scope for the initial release and may be added later.

open_questions.text: Which payment provider should be used when online payment is introduced?

follow_up_questions: Which payment provider should be integrated for future online payments?
What measurable target defines acceptable mobile loading speed?

utterances.speaker: Client

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Yes, an admin page should let staff edit menu items and prices.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: The site must load fast on mobile because most customers use phones.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: I want a clean and modern design, and maybe online payment later.

utterances.speaker: PM
utterances.text: Do you already have a preferred payment provider?

conversation:
Client: Our school clubs need an app to publish weekly meeting schedules.
Client: Club leaders should create and update events.
Client: Students should join events and receive reminder notifications.
Client: It should be easy to use for freshmen.
Client: Maybe attendance analytics can be added later.
PM: Who is allowed to cancel events?
Client: Only club leaders and the student council should cancel events.

gold.project_summary: School clubs need schedule publishing and event participation features with role-based cancellation rules and unresolved reminder details.

functional_requirements.text: The app publishes weekly club meeting schedules.
Club leaders can create and update events.
Students can join events.
Students receive reminder notifications for joined events.

non_functional_requirements.text: The app should be easy to use for freshmen.

constraints.text: Only club leaders and student council members may cancel events.

assumptions.text: Attendance analytics is deferred to a later phase.

open_questions.text: Which notification channel should be used for reminders?

follow_up_questions: Should reminder notifications be sent via push, email, or messaging apps?
What usability acceptance criteria define 'easy to use for freshmen'?

utterances.text: Our school clubs need an app to publish weekly meeting schedules.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Club leaders should create and update events.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Students should join events and receive reminder notifications.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: It should be easy to use for freshmen.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Maybe attendance analytics can be added later.

utterances.speaker: PM
utterances.text: Who is allowed to cancel events?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Only club leaders and the student council should cancel events.

conversation:
Client: We need a web system for a dental clinic where patients can book appointments.
Client: Receptionists must see daily bookings and mark no-shows.
Client: It has to work on old office PCs with slow internet.
Client: We cannot exceed a 3 month timeline.
Client: It should feel simple and user-friendly.
PM: Do patients need SMS reminders?
Client: Yes, but we have not chosen an SMS vendor yet.

gold.project_summary: Dental clinic needs appointment booking and receptionist workflows with performance and schedule constraints plus unresolved SMS integration details.

functional_requirements.text: Patients can book appointments online.
Receptionists can view daily bookings.
Receptionists can mark no-shows.
Patients receive SMS reminders for appointments.

non_functional_requirements.text: The system should work on old office PCs with slow internet.
The interface should be simple and user-friendly.

constraints.text: Delivery timeline must not exceed three months.

assumptions.text: SMS vendor selection will happen after initial requirements drafting.

open_questions.text: Which SMS vendor will be used for reminders?
What measurable criteria define 'simple and user-friendly'?

follow_up_questions: Which SMS provider should be integrated first?
What concrete usability metric will be used for acceptance?

utterances.text: We need a web system for a dental clinic where patients can book appointments.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Receptionists must see daily bookings and mark no-shows.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: It has to work on old office PCs with slow internet.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: We cannot exceed a 3 month timeline.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: It should feel simple and user-friendly.

utterances.speaker: PM
utterances.text: Do patients need SMS reminders?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Yes, but we have not chosen an SMS vendor yet.

conversation:
Client: I run a gym and need members to book classes from mobile.
Client: Trainers should set class capacity and mark attendance.
Client: We must use our existing Google Workspace login.
Client: Booking confirmation should arrive within a few seconds.
Client: Nice to have a leaderboard later.
PM: Should members be able to cancel bookings anytime?
Client: Cancellation policy is not decided yet.

gold.project_summary: Gym needs mobile class booking with trainer management, SSO integration, and unresolved cancellation rules.

functional_requirements.text: Members can book gym classes from mobile devices.
Trainers can set class capacity.
Trainers can mark class attendance.

non_functional_requirements.text: Booking confirmation should be delivered within a few seconds.

constraints.text: Authentication must use the existing Google Workspace login system.

assumptions.text: Leaderboard functionality is deferred to a future phase.

open_questions.text: What cancellation policy should apply to member bookings?

follow_up_questions: What cancellation window and penalties should be enforced?
Do cancellation limits differ by membership tier?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: We must use our existing Google Workspace login.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Booking confirmation should arrive within a few seconds.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Nice to have a leaderboard later.

utterances.speaker: PM
utterances.text: Should members be able to cancel bookings anytime?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Cancellation policy is not decided yet.

conversation:
Client: We need a platform for volunteer tutors to post available time slots.
Client: Parents should request slots and receive approval messages.
Client: The first version is web only, not iOS or Android apps.
Client: It should look modern.
PM: Do you need payment processing?
Client: No payment for now because sessions are free.
PM: How quickly should approval messages be sent?
Client: Fast, but I do not have an exact target yet.

gold.project_summary: Volunteer tutoring platform needs scheduling and approvals with web-only scope and unresolved message latency targets.

functional_requirements.text: Volunteer tutors can post available time slots.
Parents can request tutoring slots.
Parents receive approval messages for slot requests.

non_functional_requirements.text: The interface should have a modern visual style.
Approval messages should be delivered quickly.

constraints.text: The first release is web-only and excludes iOS and Android apps.
Payment processing is excluded from current scope.

assumptions.text: Sessions remain free in the first release.

open_questions.text: What exact response-time target defines 'fast' approval messaging?

follow_up_questions: What numerical latency SLA should approval messages meet?
Will payment processing stay out of scope for at least the next milestone?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Parents should request slots and receive approval messages.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: The first version is web only, not iOS or Android apps.

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: It should look modern.

utterances.speaker: PM
utterances.text: Do you need payment processing?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: No payment for now because sessions are free.

utterances.speaker: PM
utterances.text: How quickly should approval messages be sent?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Fast, but I do not have an exact target yet.

conversation:
Client: The university library needs a room reservation tool.
Client: Students should see room availability and reserve by time block.
Client: Librarians must be able to block rooms for maintenance.
Client: The service should be reliable during exam week.
Client: Maybe we can add QR check-in later.
PM: Should reservations auto-expire if students do not check in?
Client: Yes, but we have not decided the timeout.
Client: We also must keep data on school servers only.

gold.project_summary: Library room reservation tool needs core booking workflows, maintenance controls, and data-hosting constraints with open policy details.

functional_requirements.text: Students can view room availability and reserve rooms by time block.
Librarians can block rooms for maintenance.
Reservations auto-expire when students fail to check in.

non_functional_requirements.text: The service should be reliable during exam week.

constraints.text: All reservation data must remain on school-hosted servers.

assumptions.text: QR check-in will be implemented in a later phase.

open_questions.text: What timeout duration should trigger reservation auto-expiration?
What reliability target is expected during exam week?

follow_up_questions: What exact no-show timeout should be configured?
What uptime or failure-rate target defines reliability during exam week?

utterances.speaker: Client
utterances.text: Librarians must be able to block rooms for maintenance.

utterances.text: Maybe we can add QR check-in later.

utterances.text: Should reservations auto-expire if students do not check in?
